repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

`vim.lsp.enable()` skips buffers with `buftype` set. LSPs targeting buftypes like `help` never attach, even when `filetype` matches.

The original guard was added in #31031, with @mfussenegger [acknowledging](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/31031#discussion_r1868157118) a dedicated parameter might be warranted — deferred for the first version.

## Motivation

Mathias correctly predicted this to rarely ever be a problem. Now, with the advent of [`vimdoc-language-server`](https://vimdoc-language-server.barrettruth.com), there is a use case for it.

## Solution

Add `buftypes` to `vim.lsp.Config`. When `nil` (default), no buftype restriction is applied — matching how `filetypes = nil` works. Specify a table to restrict attachment to specific buffer types. Moves the check from `lsp_enable_callback` into `can_start` so each config decides independently.

```lua
vim.lsp.config('vimdoc_ls', {
  buftypes = { 'help' },
})
```

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
  if type(config.buftypes) == 'table' and not vim.tbl_contains(allowed_buftypes, vim.bo[bufnr].buftype) then
    return false
  end
```

I find this (which is what the guard below does) easier to follow.

comment_regular_data.body: Thank you for the prompt feedback @MariaSolOs! I have implemented your fixes.

I should have marked this as needs-discussion. I was hoping to see more comments from actual use-cases / motivations. And this doesn't seem like something urgent enough for Nvim 0.12.

Regarding the current default of `buftype == ""`, we could simply expand the default to `buftype == "" || buftype == "help"`. If existing lsp configs match `filetype='help'` that presumably means they are wanted there.

The reason for the current `buftype=""` restriction is to avoid attaching to ephemeral / plugins-specific buffers.

Correct me if wrong...

title: feat(lsp): add `buftypes` field to `vim.lsp.Config`
